title: Cauliflower — Nuwara Eliya skips basal urea (soil-chemistry rule)
body: HORDI publishes two cauliflower fertilizer schedules with a clean agronomic reason behind the split. Nuwara Eliya district skips basal urea entirely — basal is TSP 270 + MOP 75 kg/ha only, then urea 110 kg/ha at weeks 2, 5, and 8. Other districts (up-country outside Nuwara Eliya, and lower elevations) use the standard basal Urea 110 + TSP 270 + MOP 75, then urea 110 at week 3, then urea 110 + MOP 75 at week 6. The reason: Nuwara Eliya's organic-rich soils already mineralise enough N to cover the first 2 weeks; adding basal urea there causes excessive vegetative growth, delays curd initiation, and pushes yield down by 15–25%. Elsewhere the soils need the basal kick-start. Variety choice (Early Phenomenal or Early Patna) is the same across districts; the fertilizer difference is what matters.
quote: Fertilizer Schedule (Nuwara Eliya District): Basal: TSP 270 kg/ha, MOP 75 kg/ha; 2 weeks post-transplant: Urea 110 kg/ha; 5 weeks post-transplant: Urea 110 kg/ha, MOP 75 kg/ha; 8 weeks post-transplant: Urea 110 kg/ha. Fertilizer Schedule (Other Districts): Basal: Urea 110 kg/ha, TSP 270 kg/ha, MOP 75 kg/ha; 3 weeks post-transplant: Urea 110 kg/ha; 6 weeks post-transplant: Urea 110 kg/ha, MOP 75 kg/ha.
